---
title: "Gender Disparities in Hiring & Political Influence"
subtitle: "A Data-Driven Analysis of the 2024 Job Market"
author: "Group B"
date: today
format: html
---

## Overview

This project examines gender based disparities in hiring patterns across
industries and explores how political influence measured by state political
lean (red vs. blue) shapes employment outcomes for men and women in 2024.

Using the Lightcast dataset as our baseline, we apply a structured ETL
pipeline to extract, clean, and analyze real world job market data,
publishing our findings as an evolving research portfolio.

## Research Questions

1. How do hiring patterns differ for men vs. women across industries?
2. Do gender based employment disparities vary between red and blue states?

## Project Status

> This site is updated weekly as analysis progresses.
> Check back for new findings, visualizations, and insights.

| Week | Milestone |
|------|-----------|
| 1 | Repo setup, site initialized, ETL pipeline designed |
| 2 | Data extraction, column selection |
| ... | ... |
